title: andropogon tener subvar. pilosus
intro: Andropogon tener subvar. pilosus, also known as false bluestem or panic grass, is a type of grass native to North America and is commonly found in the prairies and grasslands of the United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat skin irritations and wounds due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Andropogon tener subvar. pilosus include its use in reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and soothing skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves by drying the leaves and stems, then steeping them in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil to create a topical application.
